title: Steakhouse Sirloin Tips
subtitle: Marinated sirloin tips with a hard sear and butter finish.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Dale's is salty — go light on added salt and heavy on black pepper.
items: 2 lbs beef sirloin tips
¼ cup Dale's marinade
1–2 tbsp olive oil
2–3 cloves garlic, minced or paste
Black pepper, heavy
Salt, light (optional)
2 tbsp butter, for finishing
ingredients.callout: Done when: deep brown crust, slightly under desired doneness when pulled — carryover finishes it.

body.html: Sirloin tips marinated in Dale's, olive oil, and garlic, then hit with a high-heat cast-iron sear and finished with a butter baste. Simple process — the marinade and crust do the work.

items: Marinate 2–4 hours in the fridge
Temper 15–20 min before cooking
Pat dry — dry surface = real crust
Sear hard on both sides without moving
Butter baste with garlic at the end
Rest briefly before serving

body.html: Don't crowd the pan. Crowding drops temp and steams instead of sears — cook in batches if needed.

items: Add sirloin tips to a zip bag.
Pour in Dale's marinade, olive oil, garlic, and a heavy hit of black pepper.
Seal with some air trapped and massage until evenly coated.
Refrigerate 2–4 hours, flipping once if you can.

body.html: Skip added salt — Dale's brings plenty. Taste after cooking if needed.

slides.title: Temper & Dry
slides.checkboxLabel: Remove from fridge 15–20 min; pat dry

items: Pull meat from the fridge 15–20 minutes before cooking.
Remove from the bag and pat dry with paper towels — leave just a slight sheen.
Heat cast iron over high heat until very hot.

body.html: Dry = crust. Wet meat steams in the pan and won't brown properly.

slides.title: Sear — Side 1
slides.checkboxLabel: Add oil, sear 2–3 min without moving

items: Add a small amount of oil to the hot pan.
Add meat in a single layer.
Sear 2–3 minutes without moving.

body.html: Don't touch it. Moving early tears the crust before it sets.

items: Flip the pieces.
Sear the second side 2–3 minutes.
If pieces are uneven, remove smaller ones early.

slides.title: Butter Finish
slides.checkboxLabel: Reduce heat; add butter + garlic; baste 30–60 sec

items: Reduce heat slightly.
Add butter and garlic to the pan.
Spoon the melted butter over the meat for 30–60 seconds.
Pull meat slightly under your target doneness — it keeps cooking off heat.

body.html: Best at medium-rare to medium — don't push past medium or the texture suffers.

items: Transfer to a plate and rest briefly before serving.
Serve as-is or slice against the grain.

body.html: Built for high heat and a strong sear. Don't overthink it.
